name: Russia
overview: Russia's Federal Law No. 152-FZ on Personal Data (2006) is a comprehensive but state-centric regime enforced by Roskomnadzor, notable for its citizen-data localisation rule (databases of Russian citizens' data must be primarily stored in Russia) and notification-based cross-border transfer controls tightened in 2022-2023. 2024 amendments introduced turnover-based fines for data leaks effective 30 May 2025.

regulator.name: Roskomnadzor (Federal Service for Supervision of Communications, IT and Mass Media)

legal_instruments.title: Federal Law No. 152-FZ on Personal Data
legal_instruments.issuing_authority: State Duma / President of the Russian Federation
legal_instruments.summary: Comprehensive law: consent-centric processing, operator notification to Roskomnadzor, localisation of Russian citizens' data (Art. 18(5)), cross-border transfer notification/approval regime, breach reporting to Roskomnadzor (24/72 hours), and biometric data restrictions.

legal_instruments.title: Federal Law No. 420-FZ (2024) — turnover-based fines for personal data leaks
legal_instruments.issuing_authority: State Duma / President of the Russian Federation
legal_instruments.summary: Amends the Code of Administrative Offences: fines for large-scale leaks up to RUB 15 million (first offence) and 1-3% of annual turnover (min RUB 20 million, max RUB 500 million) for repeats; criminal liability for trafficking in personal data introduced in parallel.

developments.title: Turnover-based leak fines take effect
developments.summary: The new administrative penalty scale for personal data breaches became effective, dramatically raising financial exposure for operators suffering repeat leaks.

developments.title: Cross-border transfer notification regime tightens
developments.summary: Amendments requiring prior notification (and for non-adequate countries, Roskomnadzor assessment with power to prohibit) for cross-border transfers became fully operative.

penalties: From 30 May 2025: leak fines up to RUB 15M (first) and 1-3% of turnover (RUB 20M-500M) for repeat large leaks; localisation violations up to RUB 18M; criminal penalties for unlawful data trafficking.
cross_border: Prior notification to Roskomnadzor required; transfers to non-Convention-108/non-adequate countries subject to assessment and possible prohibition. Primary databases of Russian citizens' data must be located in Russia.
